name: SMS Input - Inspection
description: Publicly available inspection data used for the current month's SMS calculations. Contains identifying information for each inspection including U.S. DOT#, Report Number, Inspection Date, State, and Vehicle Information. Also includes relationship of each inspection to SMS BASIC information and violation counts by BASIC.

fields.description: Unique identification number for each inspection
Unique report number of the inspection
State abbreviation indicating the state the inspector is from. 'US' means federal inspectors.
Unique number assigned to a company by the DOT
The date of the inspection
ID indicating the level of inspection: 1 - Full, 2 - Walk-around, 3 - Driver-Only, 4 - Special Study, 5 - Terminal, 6 - Radioactive Materials
State abbreviation indicating where the inspection occurred
Time weight of the inspection
Number of Out-Of-Service violations related to Driver
Number of Out-Of-Service violations related to vehicle
Number of violations related to Hazardous Materials
Total number of Out-Of-Service violations
Total number of Out-Of-Service violations related to Hazardous Materials
TRUE means the hazmat placard is required
Description of the type of the main unit
Description of the make of the main unit
License plate of the main unit
License state of the main unit
Vehicle Identification Number of the main unit
Decal number of the main unit
Description of the type of the secondary unit
Description of the make of the secondary unit
License plate of the secondary unit
License state of the secondary unit
Vehicle Identification Number of the secondary unit
Decal number of the secondary unit
TRUE means the inspection is identified as relevant to the Unsafe Driving BASIC
TRUE means the inspection is identified as relevant to the Hours-of-Service Compliance BASIC
TRUE means the inspection is identified as relevant to the Driver Fitness BASIC
TRUE means the inspection is identified as relevant to the Controlled Substances/Alcohol BASIC
TRUE means the inspection is identified as relevant to the Vehicle Maintenance BASIC
TRUE means the inspection is identified as relevant to the Hazardous Materials Compliance BASIC
Total number of BASIC violations
Number of Unsafe Driving BASIC violations
Number of Hours-of-Service Compliance BASIC violations
Number of Driver Fitness BASIC violations
Number of Controlled Substances/Alcohol BASIC violations
Number of Vehicle Maintenance BASIC violations
